title: excoecaria oppositifolia var. crenulata
intro: Excoecaria oppositifolia var. crenulata, also known as the poison tree, is a plant species native to tropical regions, characterized by its toxic sap and dark green leaves. The medicinal properties of Excoecaria oppositifolia var. crenulata are attributed to its presence of toxic compounds, which have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The health benefits of this plant include its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, reduce inflammation, and prevent infections. Excoecaria oppositifolia var. crenulata can be prepared into herbal remedies, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, although its use requires caution due to its toxic nature.
